Bus Timings

F16 Bus Schedule Onpassive Terminus to FARNEK Staff Accommodation and Return

August 22, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Dubai Bus F16 provides an essential transportation link between various residential and commercial areas in Dubai. It caters primarily to commuters traveling between the Onpassive Terminus and FARNEK Staff Accommodation, serving multiple stops along the route.

Bus Timings

At the Onpassive Terminus departure point, the F16 bus operates at frequent intervals throughout the day. Services start as early as 08:17 in the morning and continue until late at night, with the last bus departing at 00:10. This schedule ensures convenient access for commuters at various hours.

From Onpassive Terminus From FARNEK Staff Accommodation

Download PDF Bus F16

Bus F16 Timetable

To view the full schedule, you can visit the official RTA website or follow this link to access the F16 Bus Timings.

Bus Route

The F16 route includes stops at the following locations:

  1. Onpassive Terminus

  2. Cement Factory

  3. Collapse

  4. Al Waaha Street

  5. Asco Labour Camp

  6. Taj Al Madina Supermarket

  7. Al Futtaim Private Staff Accommodation

  8. FARNEK Staff Accommodation

  9. Credence High School

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Emirates Post Office

  2. New Housing Junction

  3. Latifa Bint Hamdan Street 2

  4. Onpassive Terminus

Also See: Comprehensive Guide to Dubai Bus 27: Route and Stops

Bus Ticket Price

Fares for Dubai’s F16 bus depend on zones traveled, not a flat rate. This means you can expect to pay the minimum fare for a single zone trip, which is around AED 3.

Bus Booking

This ensures travelers can plan their journeys efficiently and make informed choices about their transportation needs in Dubai.

Journey Experience

The F16 bus route from Onpassive Terminus to FARNEK Staff Accommodation and back offers a long but insightful journey through a mix of urban and industrial areas in Dubai. Starting at Onpassive Terminus, the bus navigates through bustling commercial districts, showcasing Dubai’s modern infrastructure.

As the journey progresses, it transitions into quieter, more industrial zones, providing a glimpse into the city’s expanding workforce hubs and staff accommodation areas. This route highlights a different side of Dubai, focusing less on tourism and more on the practical infrastructure that supports the city’s workforce. Despite its length, the F16 route offers a unique perspective on how Dubai balances its vibrant urban life with the needs of its working population.

Read More: Complete Guide to Dubai Bus 24: Route and Stops

Traveling with Careem

With the Careem app, travelers can conveniently reach Taj Al Madina Supermarket, the farthest point on the F16 bus route originating from Onpassive Terminus. This route provides a cost-effective transportation option with fares ranging from AED 23 to 27, ensuring affordability for commuters accessing key stops such as Al Waaha Street, Asco Labour Camp, and Emirates Post Office along the way. Careem’s accessibility enhances the convenience of navigating Dubai’s transport network, catering to diverse travel needs throughout the day.

Dubai Bus F16 serves as a reliable mode of public transport connecting residential areas with essential amenities and commercial centers, contributing to the city’s comprehensive transport network. For up-to-date information and any inquiries regarding the F16 bus service, passengers are encouraged to contact the RTA or visit their official website.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: August 22, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
